references.title: Clickstream Knowledge Tracing: Modeling How Students Answer Interactive Online Questions

extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Most KT approaches only consider answers to model learner knowledge without considering how they got to the answer, which provides a naive and incomplete picture of a learner’s knowledge.
extracted.Novelty of Model: Model proposed is an expansion of DKT that considers the clickstream of the in-question activities as an integral part of the knowledge model.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Answer states are stored in JSON.

All data is normalized and, if necessary, One-Hot Encoded. 

Timestamps are converted to time intervals (in logathmic form) and each partial score is considered as categorical. 

Data is used tp build an embedding representation using an Autoencoder.
extracted.Models: Clickstream Knowledge Tracing-PT, CKT-FT, CKT-E2E, DKT
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model:
Math Unit:

{AUC: 0.8313 - CKT-FT, }

Question ID:

Math Dimension:
extracted.Comments:
CKT is presented under 3 paradigms:

Frozen Pre-trained weights - CKT-PT

Fine-tuned rpe-trained weights - CKT-FT

Trained from Scratch - CKT-E2E

Authors experiment their results based on 3 forms of indexing for knowledge components:

1. At the Question ID level, 

2. At the Math Unit, 

3. At the Math Dimension

Authors also identify what is the minimum number of questions a learner needs to answer to address the cold-start problem and find that CKT-PT reequires between 5-8% less questions than DKT.
extracted.Limitations: Comparison to DKT only, discarding other potential approaches.

annotations.text: KT often revolves around using previous questions to predict whether the next question will be correctly answered. One of the main drawbacks of the traditional KT approaches is that it only considers correctedness of previous questions, but not the rationale that led to a specific answer.

Authors use unsupervised learning on clickstream data to identify different patterns of question-answering behavior. This is followed by Clickstream KT, a model that is standard KT that also considers clickstream data within its parameters. Results show that inserting clickstream leads to better performance of the KT models.
